subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
indicator_description: Apply the understanding of place value to read and write in number quantities over 1,000,000,000.
examplars:
E.g.1. Read and write numbers in words and vice versa.
(i) 2408321: Two million, four hundred and eight thousand, three hundred and twenty-one.
(ii) the numeral part of the serial number on a currency note TD1567451, i.e., 1567451: One million, five hundred and sixty seven thousand, four hundred and fifty-one.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
indicator_description: Skip count forwards and backwards in 10,000s, 100,000s, 500,000s, etc.
examplars:
E.g.1 Count forward in 500000s up to the fifth number.
E.g. 2. Count backwards in 100,500s up to the fifth number.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
indicator_description: Compare and order whole numbers using ">, <, and ="
examplars: E.g. 1 Identify numbers which are 100,000, 1500,000, etc. more or less than given 8 to 9-digit number.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
indicator_description: Express integers of any size into standard form
examplars:
E.g.1 Write integers as a power of 10:
E.g. 2. Write multiples of 10 in standard form:
E.g.3. Write integers in standard form:

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
indicator_description: Express integers in a given number of significant and decimal places
examplars:
E.g.1. Express any given integer to a given number of significant figures.
E.g. 2. Express 975.8674, correct to (i) two decimal places; (ii) three decimal places.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and the use of place value for expressing quantities in standard form and rounding numbers and decimals to significant figures and a given number of decimal places
indicator_description: Create and solve word or real-life problems on place values
examplars: E.g. 1 Solve word or story problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.2 Apply the concepts and vocabulary of sets on sets of factors of numbers to identify perfect squares, determine their square root and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of two sets
indicator_description: Use the concept of sets to identify perfect squares and determine the square roots. Use the knowledge on sets and sets of factors of numbers to solve problems
examplars:
E.g. 1. Identify perfect squares or perfect numbers.
E.g. 2. Use the knowledge on odd numbers to determine the square root of perfect numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.1.2 Apply the concepts and vocabulary of sets on sets of factors of numbers to identify perfect squares, determine their square root and solve real life problems involving union and intersection of two sets
indicator_description: Use the knowledge on sets and sets of factors of numbers to solve real life problems involving union and intersection
examplars:
E.g. 1. Identify the set of factors of given numbers.
E.g. 2. Solve story and real-life problems involving union and intersection of sets

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
indicator_description: Multiply and divide by power of 10 including decimals and the benchmark fractions
examplars:
E.g.1. Recall multiplication facts up to 144 and related division facts.
E.g.2. Recall decimal names of the benchmark fractions converted to decimals or percentages (and vice versa).
E.g. 3. Determine a product when a decimal number is a multiple of 10, 100, 1000, 1/10 , 1/100, 1/1000, etc.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties to do calculation
examplars: E.g. 1. Apply halving and doubling to determine the product given product of two given numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.1 Apply mental mathematics strategies and number properties used to solve problems
indicator_description: Apply mental mathematics strategies to solve word problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Play mental maths word games.
E.g.2. Play mental maths word games: - should provide opportunities for learners to use mental strategies, short methods and sundry tables to develop fluency in solving problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.2 Apply the understanding of the addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers within 10,000, and (ii) decimals up to 1/1000, to solve problems and round answers to given decimal places.
indicator_description: Add and subtract more than four-digit numbers.
examplars: E.g.1. Use partitioning (or expanded form) and place value system to add and subtract whole and decimal numbers.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.2 Apply the understanding of the addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers within 10,000, and (ii) decimals up to 1/1000, to solve problems and round answers to given decimal places.
indicator_description: Multiply or divide multi-digit numbers by 2- and 3-digit numbers.
examplars:
E.g.1 Use the area model (Expand and Box method) to multiply and divide efficiently.
E.g.2. Multiply whole numbers using the vertical place value method
E.g.3 Multiply whole numbers using the lattice method
E.g.4 Investigate and determine basic division facts including divisibility test

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.2 Apply the understanding of the addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of (i) whole numbers within 10,000, and (ii) decimals up to 1/1000, to solve problems and round answers to given decimal places.
indicator_description: Create and solve story problems involving decimals on the four basic operations.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Solve word problems
E.g.2 Solve word problems on data presented in a table.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of the laws of indices in solving problems (including real life problems) involving powers of natural numbers
indicator_description: Identify and explain the laws of indices
examplars: E.g.1 State the Laws of Indices.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of the laws of indices in solving problems (including real life problems) involving powers of natural numbers
indicator_description: Apply the laws of indices to simplify and evaluate numbers involving powers of numbers. (PEDMAS)
examplars: E.g.1 Use the laws of indices to solve problems involving powers of number.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of the laws of indices in solving problems (including real life problems) involving powers of natural numbers
indicator_description: Solve exponential equations
examplars: E.g.1 Solve these equations

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.2.3 Demonstrate understanding and the use of the laws of indices in solving problems (including real life problems) involving powers of natural numbers
indicator_description: Solve real life problems involving powers of natural numbers.
examplars: E.g.1: Solve real-life problems on populations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.3.1 Apply the understanding of operation on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
indicator_description: Review fractions and solve problems involving basic operations on fractions
examplars:
E.g. 1. Review the concept of fractions.
E.g. 2. Review the basic operations on fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.3.1 Apply the understanding of operation on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
indicator_description: Add and/or subtract, multiply and/or divide given fractions, by using the principle of the order of operations (the rule of BODMAS or PEMDAS), and apply the understanding to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Use the order of operations (BODMAS or PEDMAS) to simplify whole number expressions with more than two operations.
E.g. 2. Use the order of operations (BODMAS or PEDMAS) to simplify whole number expressions with more than two operations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.3.1 Apply the understanding of operation on fractions to solve problems involving fractions of given quantities and round the results to given decimal and significant places
indicator_description: Review word problems involving basic operations on fractions and related concepts.
examplars: E.g. 1. Solve word problems involving fractions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.4.1Demonstrate an understanding of ratio, rate and proportions and use it these to solve real-world mathematical problems
indicator_description: Use ratio reasoning to convert measurement units; manipulate and transform units appropriately when multiplying or dividing quantities.
examplars:
E.g.1 Convert one unit of measure to another using ratio reasoning.
E.g.2 Manipulate and use units appropriately to solve problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.4.1Demonstrate an understanding of ratio, rate and proportions and use it these to solve real-world mathematical problems
indicator_description: Solve unit rate problems including those involving unit pricing and constant speed; and speed translation.
examplars:
E.g.1 If it took 7 hours to mow 4 lawns, then at that rate, how many lawns could be mowed in 35 hours?
E.g.2 Salamatu is a drummer for a band. She burns 756 calories while drumming for 3 hours. How many calories does Salamatu burn per hour?

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.4.1Demonstrate an understanding of ratio, rate and proportions and use it these to solve real-world mathematical problems
indicator_description: Apply the knowledge of speed to draw and interpret travel graphs or distance-time graphs.
examplars:
E.g.1 Draw a graph for a passage on a distance time graph.
E.g.2 Interpret a given travel graph

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.4.1Demonstrate an understanding of ratio, rate and proportions and use it these to solve real-world mathematical problems
indicator_description: Recognise and represent proportional relationships between quantities by deciding whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship.
examplars:
E.g.1 Use given tables to check proportional relationships.
E.g.2 Use graphs to check proportional and non-proportional relationship

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.1.4.1Demonstrate an understanding of ratio, rate and proportions and use it these to solve real-world mathematical problems
indicator_description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships.
examplars:
E.g.1 Find the constant of proportionality from a table of values, equation and a graph.
E.g.2 An ant travels 9/8 inches in 45 seconds and 27/8 inches in 2 minutes and 15 seconds. What is the constant of proportionality?
E.g.3 Find constant proportionality from given table and graph.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to draw table of values for a linear relation, graph the relation in a number plane, determine the gradient of the line and use it to write equation of a line of the form y = mx + c.
indicator_description: Calculate the gradient of a line and use it to write equation of a line of the form y = mx + c.
examplars:
E.g.1 Explain the concept of gradient using real life examples and to discover the practical meaning of gradient.
E.g.2 Determine the formula for calculating the gradient of a line.
E.g.3 Determine the gradient when given two coordinates.
E.g.4 Determine the gradient of a straight line when its equation is given.
E.g.5 Determine the gradient from a graph.
E.g.6 Determine the slope-intercept form of the equation of a straight line
E.g.7 Determine the point-slope form of the equation of a straight line

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to draw table of values for a linear relation, graph the relation in a number plane, determine the gradient of the line and use it to write equation of a line of the form y = mx + c.
indicator_description: Use graph of a linear relation to determine subsequent missing elements in the ordered pairs of the relation.
examplars:
E.g.1 Use information from a graph to find missing elements
E.g.2 Use information from a graph to find missing elements:

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.1.1 Demonstrate the ability to draw table of values for a linear relation, graph the relation in a number plane, determine the gradient of the line and use it to write equation of a line of the form y = mx + c.
indicator_description: Use graphs of linear relations to solve real life problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 Draw graphs for real life problems.
E.g.2 Nhyira paints portraits of people for a living. The graph below shows how much she charges based on how long it takes her to paint the portrait. Use the graph to answer the questions that follow.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Solve problems involving algebraic expressions (including multiplication of binomial expressions) factorise given expressions and substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Use the distributive property to remove brackets and solve multiplication of binomial expression.
examplars:
E.g.1 Expand these expressions
E.g.2 Multiply binomial expressions

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Solve problems involving algebraic expressions (including multiplication of binomial expressions) factorise given expressions and substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Perform addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of algebraic expressions including fractions.
examplars:
E.g.1 Solve problems based on multiplication and division of algebraic fractions.
E.g.2 Solve problems based on addition and subtraction of algebraic fractions

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Solve problems involving algebraic expressions (including multiplication of binomial expressions) factorise given expressions and substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions including fractions and use these to solve problems.
examplars: E.g. Simplify, then substitute in the value to evaluate the following expressions:

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.2.1 Solve problems involving algebraic expressions (including multiplication of binomial expressions) factorise given expressions and substitute values to evaluate algebraic expressions.
indicator_description: Factorise given expressions involving the four operations and use the experiences gained to solve problems.
examplars: E.g.1 Factorise the following expressions using Common factors and Method of grouping.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear inequalities of the form x + a ≥ b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear inequalities and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Translate word problems into linear inequalities in one variable and vice versa
examplars: E.g.1 Make mathematical sentences involving linear inequalities from word problems.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear inequalities of the form x + a ≥ b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear inequalities and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Solve simple linear inequalities
examplars: E.g.1 Use the idea of balancing to solve simple linear inequalities.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.2.3.1 Demonstrate an understanding of linear inequalities of the form x + a ≥ b (where a and b are integers) by modelling problems as a linear inequalities and solving the problems concretely, pictorially, and symbolically.
indicator_description: Determine solution sets of simple linear inequalities in given domains
examplars: E.g.1 Find solution sets for the following linear inequalities

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and use of the relationship between parallel lines and alternate and corresponding angles and use the sum of angles in a triangle to deduce the angle sum in any polygon.
indicator_description: Draw and determine the values of alternate and corresponding angles
examplars:
E.g.1. Draw the diagram and calculate the values of the angles
E.g. 2 Calculate the value of the angles a, b, c, and d

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.1.1 Demonstrate understanding and use of the relationship between parallel lines and alternate and corresponding angles and use the sum of angles in a triangle to deduce the angle sum in any polygon.
indicator_description: Determine the values of angles in a triangle using knowledge of the sum of interior angles in a triangle and other properties.
examplars:
E.g. 1 Calculate the values of y and the angles in the triangle
E.g. 2 Deduce the formula for the sum of interior angles in a polygon and determine the value of an angle in a regular hexagon.
E.g. 3 Use the formula for finding the sum of interior angles in a polygon (n - 2)*180 to determine the value of x in the hexagon.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.1.2 Demonstrate the ability to perform geometric constructions of the angles (75˚, 105˚, 60˚, 135˚ and 150˚), and construct triangles and find locus of points under given conditions.
indicator_description: Construct and bisect angles of 120˚, 105˚, 135˚ and 150˚
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform geometric construction of an angle 120˚.
E.g.2: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform geometric construction of an angle of 150˚.
E.g.3: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform the geometric construction of an angle 135˚.
E.g.4: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform geometric construction of an angle of 105˚.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.1.2 Demonstrate the ability to perform geometric constructions of the angles (75˚, 105˚, 60˚, 135˚ and 150˚), and construct triangles and find locus of points under given conditions.
indicator_description: Construct scalene triangles, isosceles triangles, equilateral triangles, obtuse-angled triangle, and acute-angled triangles in different orientations under given conditions.
examplars:
E.g.1: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct an equilateral triangle when a side is given.
E.g.2 : Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct an equilateral triangle using point A as a centre and constructing an arc.
E.g.3: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform geometric construction of an isosceles right-angled triangle when the base line is given.
E.g.4: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform geometric construction of an isosceles triangle when all the sides are given.
E.g.5: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to perform geometric construction of an isosceles triangle when the base angles and base side are known.
E.g.6: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct acute-angled triangles, obtuse-angled triangles and right-angled triangles when a side and two angles are given.
E.g.7: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct triangles when all the sides are given.
E.g.8: Use a pair of compasses and a ruler to construct triangles when two sides and one angle are given.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.1.2 Demonstrate the ability to perform geometric constructions of the angles (75˚, 105˚, 60˚, 135˚ and 150˚), and construct triangles and find locus of points under given conditions.
indicator_description: Construct loci under given conditions including: (i) the locus of sets of points from a fixed point; (ii) the locus of points equidistant from two fixed points; (iii) the locus of points equidistant from two intersecting straight lines, and (iv) the locus of points equidistant from two parallel lines.
examplars:
E.g.1: Describe the locus of a circle by tracing the path of a point P which moves in such a way that its distance from a fixed point is always the same to construct circles.
E.g.2: Perform geometric construction to locate the centre of a circle by locating the intersection of the perpendicular bisectors of any two chords on the circle.
E.g.3: Draw circles of given radii at the points as centre and chord.
E.g.4: Construct a regular hexagon within a circle given the length of a side.
E.g.5 Use intersecting circles to construct a regular hexagon and measure it sides.
E.g.6: Construct a perpendicular bisector (mediator) as a locus and explain why the perpendicular bisector is a locus.
E.g. 7 Construct an angle bisector as a locus of points equidistant from two lines that meet and explain why the angle bisector is a locus.
E.g.8: Construct parallel lines as a locus.
E.g.9: Perform geometric constructions to prove that two given lines are parallel.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.8.3.2.1 Apply the Pythagoras theorem, the primary trigonometric ratios and the formulas for determining the area of a circle to solve real problems.
indicator_description: Use the relationship between the diameter and circumference of a circle to deduce the formula for finding its area, and use this to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1: Divide a circle into sectors then cut the sectors and arrange to form a rectangle to deduce the area of the circle.
E.g. 2 Solve problems on area of a circle.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.8.3.2.1 Apply the Pythagoras theorem, the primary trigonometric ratios and the formulas for determining the area of a circle to solve real problems.
indicator_description: Establish the relationship between the hypotenuse ‘c’ and the two other sides ‘a’ and ‘b’ of a right-angled triangle (i.e. a2 + b2 = c2) and use it to solve problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 Construct squares on the three sides of a right-angled triangle in a square grid and compare the area of the square on the hypotenuse to the squares on the other two sides.
E.g. 2 Using a pair of compasses and ruler, construct squares on the three sides of a right-angled triangle and measure the area.
E.g. 3 Solve problems involving the Pythagoras theorem.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.8.3.2.1 Apply the Pythagoras theorem, the primary trigonometric ratios and the formulas for determining the area of a circle to solve real problems.
indicator_description: Use the Pythagorean theorem to solve problems on right angled triangle.
examplars:
E.g.1 Find the altitude of an isosceles triangle.
E.g.2 Find the length of each of the diagrams indicated below.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.8.3.2.1 Apply the Pythagoras theorem, the primary trigonometric ratios and the formulas for determining the area of a circle to solve real problems.
indicator_description: Use the Pythagoras theorem to calculate the area of a triangle in real life problems.
examplars:
E.g.1 A boat travels 2m South and then 9m East. How far is the boat from its starting point?
E.g.2 Calculate length and area of picture frame triangle.
E.g.3 A ladder leans against a vertical wall.
E.g.4 Find the height, area, and perimeter of an equilateral triangle.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B.8.3.2.1 Apply the Pythagoras theorem, the primary trigonometric ratios and the formulas for determining the area of a circle to solve real problems.
indicator_description: Establish the relationship between the basic trigonometric ratios and solve problems involving right-angled triangles.
examplars:
E.g.1 Identify and recognise the three primary trigonometric ratios.
E.g. 2 Explain the angles of elevation and depression in real life situations.
E.g.3 Use trig ratios and the Pythagoras theorem to solve problems involving angles of elevation and depression.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.2.2 Demonstrate understanding of addition and subtraction of vectors and their applications in solving basic problems
indicator_description: Add, subtract and find the scalar multiplication of vectors in the component form.
examplars:
E.g1 Add vectors using the graphical method.
E.g.2 Add and subtract vectors in their corresponding components.
E.g3 Multiply a vector by a scalar.
E.g.4 Evaluate vectors with multiple operations.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.2.2 Demonstrate understanding of addition and subtraction of vectors and their applications in solving basic problems
indicator_description: Demonstrate understanding of vector equality.
examplars: E.g.1 Investigate the properties of equal vectors.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. rotation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence)
indicator_description: Understand rotation and identify real-life situations involving rotation.
examplars: E.g.1. Identify examples of rotation situations in everyday life and the nature of rotational movements as clockwise and anti-clockwise.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. rotation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence)
indicator_description: Draw rotation image in a coordinate plane and determine the angle of rotation.
examplars:
E.g. 1. Rotate a shape through a given centre of rotation and angle of rotation using rotation rules.
E.g. 2. Determine the angle of rotation using the points of an object, its images and centre.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.3.3.1 Perform a single transformation (i.e. rotation) on a 2D shape using graph paper (including technology) and describe the properties of the image under the transformation (i.e. congruence)
indicator_description: Investigate the concept of congruent shapes.
examplars: E.g. 1. Use multiple and varied examples of rotation on coordinate planes to verify congruent shapes based on their properties.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), use the data (grouped/ungrouped) to construct and interpret frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, and pictograms to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Identify types of given data including numerical, categorical, ungrouped and grouped data
examplars:
E.g. 1 Discuss, in small groups, information collected in the process of investigation which may be numeric.
E.g. 2 Discuss (in groups) information collected in the process of investigation which may be non-numeric.
E.g. 3 Group scores for 11 learners in a class test.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), use the data (grouped/ungrouped) to construct and interpret frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, and pictograms to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Select and justify a method to collect data (quantitative and qualitative) to answer a given question.
examplars:
E.g. 1- To study how eating cream crackers affects one's output of work (productivity), identify which method can be used to gather the facts.
E.g. 2 -Select any study to be undertaken and design an appropriate form to be used in collecting data.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.1.1 Select, justify, and use appropriate methods to collect data (quantitative and qualitative), use the data (grouped/ungrouped) to construct and interpret frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, and pictograms to solve and/or pose problems.
indicator_description: Organise data (grouped/ungrouped), present it in frequency tables, line graphs, pie graphs, bar graphs and/or pictographs (representations include info graphics, waffle diagrams, box and whisker plots and stem and leaf plots) and analyse it to solve and/or pose problems.
examplars:
E.g. 1 The following set of raw data shows the lengths, in millimetres, measured to the nearest mm, of 40 leaves taken from plants of a certain species.
E.g. -2 A cleaner of a small office spent GH₵120 of his salary on food; GH₵80 on rent; GH₵40 on clothing, GH₵110 on transport and saved GH₵50. Organise the data and draw a bar chart and a pie chart to represent the data.
E.g. -3 – The waffle chart shows that the average score obtained by B7 learners in a mathematics test conducted, is 64%.
E.g. 4 Make a stem and leaf plot for scores of 14 B8 learners in a test.
E.g. 5 – The stem and leaf plot shows the scores obtained by learners in a test. Use it to answer questions.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and range for grouped data and explain when it's most appropriate to use the mean, median, or mode.
indicator_description: Calculate the mean, median and mode for a given set of ungrouped data, and explain why these values may be the same or different.
examplars:
E.g. 1 The bar graph shows the sales of a small business from Monday to Friday. Calculate the mean, median and mode for amounts collected.
E. g. 2 The table below shows the area of the sitting room floors of each of 7 real estate houses in Kwashiman. Work out the mean, median, mode.
E.g. 3. The table below shows the occurrence of the data values from 1 to 7 and is represented by the corresponding bar graph.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.1.2 Demonstrate an understanding of measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and range for grouped data and explain when it's most appropriate to use the mean, median, or mode.
indicator_description: Justify a context in which the mean, median or mode is the most appropriate measure of central tendency to use when reporting findings.
examplars: E.g.1 Kojo's says his taxi makes a number of trips each day as shown in the table below. Calculate the mean, median and mode for Kojo's trips for the week.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.2.1 Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving two independent events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems.
indicator_description: Perform a probability experiment involving two independent events such as drawing coloured bottle tops from a bag with replacement and list the elements of the sample space
examplars:
E.g. 1 In an experiment, Emmanuel was asked to pick one bottle top from a bag, three times, which contains 3 red, 2 green and 1 pink bottle tops.
E.g. 2 Consider the following two events: (a) throwing of a fair six-sided die and (b) tossing a fair coin
E.g. 3 Ampofo and Serwa are two learners from a school. Ampofo walks to school daily and Serwa travels to school on a bus daily.

subject: Mathematics
content_standard: B8.4.2.1 Identify the sample space for a probability experiment involving two independent events and express the probabilities of given events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios to solve problems.
indicator_description: Express the probabilities of the events as fractions, decimals, percentages and/or ratios. e.g. by using a tree diagram, table or other graphic organiser.
examplars:
E.g. 1 The arrow on the spinner if spun twice and the number of wins recorded.
E.g. 2-A box contains 3 blue pens and 4 pink pens. A pen is taken from the box, its colour noted, and then replaced. Another pen is taken and its colour noted.
E.g. 2 A die is thrown at most three times. If 6 is scored the game stops.
